title: Payroll Services

sources.title: Third party payer arrangements – Payroll service providers and reporting agents
sources.what_it_supports: Payroll-service-provider operating boundary, employer authorization, payroll-tax filing/deposit activities, and the distinction between PSPs and reporting agents.

sources.title: 541214: Payroll Services - Census Bureau Profile
sources.what_it_supports: The NAICS 541214 definition and exclusions for CPA offices, accounting/bookkeeping/billing providers, financial-transaction processors, and non-payroll data processors.

sources.title: Reporting Agents File (RAF)
sources.what_it_supports: Reporting-agent company status, Form 8655 authorization, tax-deposit and tax-information-filing responsibilities, and the limits of this public operating evidence.

narrative.executive_view: The frozen target is a full-service payroll provider, not an accounting, PEO, employer-of-record, staffing, or software-only business. Public authorities establish the service boundary but do not provide target-band financial or transaction evidence, so this primitive packet remains deliberately unresolved where target evidence is required.
narrative.revenue_and_pass_through: No target-company accrual revenue policy, financial statements, client-funds reconciliation, or documented third-party amounts embedded in revenue were available. Client payroll and employment-tax remittances must not be substituted for recognized revenue; therefore revenue, pass-through, and the reconciled CVA denominator are MISSING.
narrative.technical_value: The frozen assumption taxonomy separates payroll operations, tax compliance, client service, implementation, sales, technology/security, and management. Its source-free cash-cost normalization and removable fractions are planning assumptions only; no evidence supports a target company's employee or contractor cash pools, and no V result is authored.
narrative.implementation: IRS material confirms that providers can prepare paychecks, employment-tax returns, deposits, and W-2/W-3 work under client authorization. That establishes the presence of regulated control steps but not a target-specific system-migration timeline, implementation cost, or achievable realization schedule; the schedule below is explicitly source-free planning judgment.
narrative.commercial_capture: No admissible target-band contracts, rate cards, retention cohorts, concentration schedules, or client migration evidence were located. Commercial retention is therefore a bounded source-free scenario judgment and does not embed execution costs, operating realization, or demand survival.
narrative.buyability: The Census definition and IRS operating descriptions distinguish the chosen payroll-provider archetype from excluded business models, but neither establishes five-year owner availability, channel access, win probability, or a credible buyer's closing and onboarding capacity. Those market-access primitives remain MISSING.
narrative.entry_and_exit: No closed, entry-equivalent lower-middle-market payroll-service transaction set with verified normalized EBITDA and downside exit support was available. The required year-five state is held at the entry reference only to prevent transformation leakage; both the entry and downside multiple remain MISSING.
narrative.operator_survival: The IRS describes payroll and tax actions performed under employer authorization and emphasizes that employers retain tax responsibility. There is no target-company service-level baseline, CVA reconciliation, client quantity record, or year-five operator-controlled quantity evidence, so the fixed-base survival basket is MISSING.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The dominant uncertainties are recognized service revenue versus client funds, employee and controllable-contractor cash costs, target-specific systems and controls, retention, sale availability, buyer access, valuation, and operator-controlled service demand. Public descriptions cannot close these target-company evidence gaps.

scenario_narratives.low: Low reflects delayed operational realization, heavier implementation investment, and weaker commercial retention within the explicitly source-free schedules. It is not a valuation, availability, or target financial forecast.
scenario_narratives.base: Base is a planning midpoint for the source-free realization, investment, and commercial schedules. All target-company financial, market-access, valuation, and survival primitives remain unresolved.
scenario_narratives.high: High permits faster realization, lower investment, and stronger commercial retention only within the stated assumption ranges. It does not assert favorable pricing, transaction access, demand growth, or multiple expansion.

recognized_revenue.rationale: No target-company TTM accrual recognized-revenue record or accounting policy was available; client payroll and tax funds are not a permissible substitute.
caveats: A positive CVA denominator cannot be inferred from payroll processed or industry receipts.

evidence.rationale: No target-company ledger, invoice, contract, or client-funds reconciliation identifies an approved pass-through amount.
caveats: No pass-through category is authored, and client custodial flows must remain outside revenue.

employee_cash_cost.accounting_basis: Source-free normalization to one dollar of unobserved target TTM recognized service revenue; this is not a target-company dollar amount.

amount.rationale: Required source-free planning normalization under the frozen assumption value basis; no payroll-provider cash-cost pool was observed.
caveats: It cannot establish a target-company dollar cost or a computable V without recognized revenue.

controllable_contractor_cash_cost.accounting_basis: Source-free normalization to one dollar of unobserved target TTM recognized service revenue; only operator-controlled delivery contractors would belong here.

amount.rationale: Required source-free planning normalization under the frozen assumption value basis; no contractor ledger distinguishes controlled delivery work from pass-through.
caveats: Documented denominator pass-through would be excluded rather than double counted here.

removable_fraction.rationale: Source-free planning judgment for avoidable payroll-production cash cost after full implementation.
caveats: Not a measured staffing reduction.

removable_fraction.rationale: Source-free planning judgment that retains tax-accountability and exception work.
caveats: Not a compliance-service-level claim.

removable_fraction.rationale: Source-free planning judgment for limited workflow-related service efficiency.
caveats: It excludes any commercial response effect.

removable_fraction.rationale: Source-free planning judgment for repeatable conversion and integration work after adoption.
caveats: One-time investment remains in K, not this fraction.

removable_fraction.rationale: Source-free planning judgment for administrative efficiency only.
caveats: It does not claim demand or retention benefit.

removable_fraction.rationale: Source-free planning judgment that preserves necessary technology, security, and vendor control work.
caveats: Platform and security costs are not pass-through deductions.

removable_fraction.rationale: Source-free planning judgment for limited coordination efficiency while retaining accountable management.
caveats: It is not a management-depth forecast.
Target-specific migration evidence is unavailable.
Not an empirical implementation forecast.
May vary with payroll-cycle controls and client interfaces.
No monotonic path is asserted beyond the interval.
No customer-demand or price effect is included.

k0.rationale: Source-free up-front change-investment fraction of full gross removable cost.
caveats: Includes no target vendor quote or observed conversion cost.
Separate from realization and commercial retention.
No operating or vendor evidence supports the amount.
No target systems roadmap was available.
Separate from any pricing or demand effect.
Not a sustained vendor-cost estimate.

c1.rationale: Source-free commercial-retention planning range after any repricing and competitive response.
caveats: No contract or retention cohort evidence is available.
Does not contain implementation investment.
No price, concentration, or churn evidence supports it.
It does not measure operator-controlled demand survival.
No favorable pricing or renewal result is assumed.

target_archetype_coverage.rationale: Frozen selected-archetype bridge: 276 base firms out of 297 normalized band firms, with frozen low and high count endpoints.
caveats: The frozen product incidence is all-size establishment evidence bridged to a firm-level EBITDA band.

five_year_sale_availability.rationale: No verified sample establishes independent selected-archetype ownership, transferability, and sale availability over five years.
caveats: Unknown owner age is not treated as availability evidence.

buyer_access_win_share.rationale: No evidence supports a credible buyer's conditional access and win share at market-clearing price.
caveats: It cannot be inferred from sale availability or a price discount.

deal_execution_capacity_5y.rationale: No buyer-specific diligence, financing, and closing capacity evidence was available.
caveats: This does not create opportunities beyond the sale and access set.

integration_onboarding_capacity_5y.rationale: No buyer-specific accountable onboarding capacity evidence was available.
caveats: This is distinct from deal-execution capacity.

buy_multiple.rationale: No closed entry-equivalent selected-archetype transaction evidence with normalized EBITDA was available.
caveats: PEO, software-only, and broad-services comparables are not substituted.

normalized_y5_operating_state.rationale: Canonical source-free state holds every specified year-five dimension at the entry reference to prevent sponsor transformation from entering P.
caveats: It provides no multiple evidence.

anchor.rationale: No entry-equivalent comparable multiple level is supported for the canonical state.
caveats: No valuation endpoint is inferred from broad comparables.

operator_controlled_value_added_demand_s.rationale: No target-company baseline service quantities, recognized revenue, pass-through boundary, or year-five operator-controlled quantities can reconcile to R_cva.

cross_checks.target_identity_preserved: The packet retains the frozen full-service-offering archetype and excludes accounting, PEO, employer-of-record, staffing, and software-only substitution.
cross_checks.pass_through_removed_once: No pass-through item is authored; client custodial and tax-remittance flows are not treated as revenue or a denominator deduction.
cross_checks.removability_only_in_v: Role fractions address only full-implementation cash-cost removability and do not include pricing, sale access, valuation, or demand survival.
cross_checks.investment_only_in_i: K is a separate source-free implementation-investment schedule and is not embedded in retention or removability.
cross_checks.commercial_only_in_c: C contains only source-free commercial retention of realized savings and excludes implementation investment, demand survival, and valuation.
cross_checks.buyer_access_separate_from_price: Access/win is unresolved separately from sale availability and does not encode a price concession.
cross_checks.p_uses_frozen_y5_state: The canonical digest binds a single entry-equivalent state with all six dimensions held at the entry reference.
cross_checks.p_excludes_c_and_survival: The downside multiple has no adjustments and explicitly excludes commercial retention, survival, implementation, V, h, buyability, and sponsor effects.
cross_checks.survival_is_constant_price_value_added: The required fixed-base assertions are retained, but the basket is MISSING because it cannot reconcile to an unobserved R_cva boundary.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: Without a positive reconciled CVA denominator, V and survival cannot be computed.
The frozen taxonomy cannot establish target cash-cost magnitude or value creation.

top_uncertain_inputs.input: Sale availability, access/win, capacity, and valuation
top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: B and P remain unavailable without admissible target-band transaction and buyer evidence.

reviewer_note: Fresh official IRS and Census sources support the payroll-provider boundary and control context only. This packet intentionally leaves target-specific financial, buyability, valuation, and survival primitives MISSING, and labels the frozen assumption-basis planning inputs as ASSUMPTION/NONE.
